effect: Vitamin K directly antagonizes warfarin anticoagulation
management: Maintain CONSISTENT vitamin K intake. Do not avoid — just keep intake stable.
mechanism: Competitive pharmacodynamic antagonism at VKORC1

effect: Calcium chelates levothyroxine → absorption reduced 20-40%
management: Separate by 4+ hours. Take levothyroxine on empty stomach first.
mechanism: GI chelation complex formation

effect: Coffee reduces levothyroxine absorption by up to 36%
management: Take levothyroxine 30-60 min before coffee.
mechanism: Unknown — possibly tannin binding or GI motility

effect: Soy reduces levothyroxine absorption
management: Separate by 4 hours.
mechanism: Phytate binding and enterohepatic circulation interference

effect: CYP3A4 intestinal inhibition → Cmax +35%, AUC +40%
management: AVOID grapefruit entirely during amlodipine therapy.
mechanism: Furanocoumarins in grapefruit irreversibly inhibit intestinal CYP3A4

effect: AUC increases 1.5-2.5x with large quantities
management: Avoid >240mL (1 cup) grapefruit juice per day.
mechanism: Intestinal CYP3A4 inhibition

effect: AUC increases 3-4x even with small grapefruit amounts
management: AVOID grapefruit entirely.
mechanism: Intestinal CYP3A4 inhibition

effect: 15mg/20mg: food INCREASES bioavailability 39% — REQUIRED for adequate exposure
management: 15mg and 20mg doses MUST be taken with a meal. 10mg dose: with or without food.
mechanism: Solubility enhancement in fed state

effect: Food reduces GI side effects and slows absorption (Cmax -17%) without reducing AUC
management: Take WITH meals (breakfast and dinner) to minimize GI adverse effects.
mechanism: Slower gastric emptying reduces peak concentration

effect: ANY food (or non-water drink) reduces bioavailability >90%
management: Take with ONLY plain water (240mL), 30-60 min before first food/drink of day. Remain upright.
mechanism: GI chelation and reduced absorption in alkaline pH

effect: Calcium/Mg++/Al+++ chelate ciprofloxacin → absorption reduced 50-90%
management: Take ciprofloxacin 2 hours before or 6 hours after dairy/antacids.
mechanism: Chelation of fluoroquinolone by divalent/trivalent cations

effect: Must be taken before meals — food reduces absorption and requires pre-meal dosing for activation
management: Take 30-60 min BEFORE breakfast (or largest meal).
mechanism: Acid activation requires parietal cell activation by meal

effect: Food slows absorption → reduces orthostatic hypotension risk
management: Take WITH food to reduce postural hypotension.
mechanism: Slower Cmax with food reduces initial vasodilation

effect: Grapefruit has minimal clinically significant effect on warfarin
management: No restriction needed for warfarin.
mechanism: Warfarin primarily CYP2C9 — not significantly CYP3A4

effect: Caffeine and theophylline are both methylxanthines — additive toxicity
management: Limit caffeine intake. Monitor for tachycardia, tremor, nausea.
mechanism: Additive PDE inhibition and adenosine antagonism

effect: Grapefruit increases alprazolam AUC ~2x → excess sedation
management: Avoid grapefruit.
mechanism: Intestinal CYP3A4 inhibition

effect: Grapefruit increases fentanyl exposure significantly → respiratory depression risk
management: Avoid grapefruit during fentanyl therapy.
mechanism: Intestinal CYP3A4 inhibition

effect: Grapefruit may increase quetiapine exposure via CYP3A4 inhibition
management: Avoid large quantities of grapefruit.
mechanism: Intestinal CYP3A4 inhibition

effect: Grapefruit increases carbamazepine levels → toxicity (diplopia, ataxia)
management: Avoid grapefruit during carbamazepine therapy.
mechanism: Intestinal CYP3A4 inhibition

effect: Protein-rich foods compete with levodopa for transport across GI and BBB via LNAA transporter
management: Take levodopa 30-60 min before meals or 1-2hr after. Low-protein diet during daytime (protein redistribution diet) helps.
mechanism: Competition for large neutral amino acid transporter (LNAA) in gut and BBB

foods_list: low-salt diet|sodium restriction|excessive sweating without replacement
effect: Sodium depletion → kidney increases lithium reabsorption → lithium toxicity
management: Maintain normal consistent sodium intake. Increase fluid intake in hot weather. Avoid crash diets.
mechanism: Renal sodium-lithium competition: proximal tubule reabsorbs lithium when sodium depleted

effect: Acute: CYP2C9 inhibition → warfarin increase. Chronic: CYP2C9 induction → warfarin decrease. Variable.
management: Maintain consistent (low) alcohol intake. Abstinence preferred. Monitor INR closely.
mechanism: Acute: competitive CYP2C9 inhibition. Chronic: CYP2C9 induction

effect: Additive hepatotoxicity — both cause liver damage; increased risk of fibrosis
management: ABSOLUTE alcohol avoidance during methotrexate therapy.
mechanism: Additive hepatocellular toxicity

foods_list: aged cheese (cheddar, blue, brie)|cured meats (salami, pepperoni)|fermented products|red wine|tap beer|soy sauce|yeast extracts
effect: MAO inhibition by linezolid + dietary tyramine → sympathomimetic crisis (hypertensive emergency, headache, palpitations)
management: Strict tyramine dietary restriction during linezolid therapy. Each meal <10mg tyramine.
mechanism: MAO-A inhibition → tyramine not metabolized in gut/liver → pressor effect

effect: Grapefruit increases sildenafil AUC up to 2x → hypotension, flushing, visual disturbance
management: Avoid grapefruit.
mechanism: Intestinal CYP3A4 inhibition

effect: Calcium, tannins, polyphenols chelate iron → absorption reduced 30-60%
management: Take iron on empty stomach with water or vitamin C (enhances absorption). Separate from calcium/tea/coffee by 2 hours.
mechanism: Chelation and tannin binding prevent iron absorption

effect: Calcium/Mg++/Al+++ chelate doxycycline → absorption reduced (less than other tetracyclines — can be taken with food)
management: Can take with food but separate from dairy/antacids by 2 hours.
mechanism: Divalent cation chelation (less severe than other tetracyclines due to higher protein binding)
